user_story: As a user, I want to start a new project and specify its operating mode (Project Mode or Trace mode), so that the project is created with the correct configuration and ready to be saved and loaded later.
true_user_story: The START NEW PROJECT function shall allow the user to create a new project.

user_story: As a user, I want to be prompted to provide the new project name and the low‑level document path when I click the "Load RTM" button, so that I can create a new project with the correct details and link it to the appropriate documentation.
true_user_story: The user shall be prompted to provide the new project name.

user_story: As a project administrator, I want to be prompted to enter the new project name, the low‑level document path, the high‑level document path, and the RTM file path, so that I can configure a new project with all required documentation references in a single, guided setup process.
true_user_story: The user shall be prompted to provide the low level document path.

user_story: As a project setup user, I want to be prompted to provide the new project name, the low‑level document path, the high‑level document path, and the RTM file path, so that I can correctly initialize and configure the project’s documentation structure.
true_user_story: The user shall be prompted to provide the high level document.

user_story: As a user, I want to be optionally prompted to select an IR method from the list of Vector‑Space Retrieval, Keyword Extraction, and Keyword Extraction‑IDF, so that I can customize the information‑retrieval process to best suit my needs.
true_user_story: The user shall optionally be prompted to select an IR Method from the following list: Vector-Space Retrieval Keyword Extraction Keyword Extraction-IDF

user_story: As a user, I want to be prompted to select a Vocabulary Base of either "Both Documents" or "Low Level Document Only" (with the default set to "Both Documents"), so that I can customize keyword extraction to the scope of documents I am working with.
true_user_story: The user shall optionally be prompted to select a Vocabulary Base of either “Both Documents” or “Low Level Document Only”.  The default value is “Both Documents”.

user_story: As a user of RETRO, I want to be prompted to select a weight option (TF‑IDF, Okapi, LTU, or PASS) whenever I choose the Keyword Extraction or Keyword Extraction‑IDF IR method, so that I can control the weighting scheme applied to the retrieved documents and tailor the results to my needs.
true_user_story: The user shall optionally be prompted to provide Weight Option from the following list: TF-IDF (Default) Okapi LTU

user_story: As a user of the search system, I want the interface to require me to select a Weight Threshold whenever I choose the IR Method ‘Keyword Extraction’ or ‘Keyword Extraction‑IDF’, so that the system has the necessary parameter to perform accurate keyword extraction.
true_user_story: If the user has selected an IR Method of Keyword Extraction or Keyword Extraction-IDF, the user must also select a Weight Threshold.

user_story: As a user, I want to be optionally prompted to provide a Thesaurus filename and a Stop Word filename, so that I can customize the thesaurus and stop‑word list used by the application.
true_user_story: The user shall optionally be prompted to provide a Thesaurus Filename.

user_story: As a user, I want to be optionally prompted to provide a thesaurus filename and a stop‑word file, so that I can customize the application’s text processing behavior without being forced to supply these files.
true_user_story: The user shall optionally be prompted to provide the Stop Word file.

user_story: As a user, I want to be optionally prompted to provide a Feedback Method, so that I can specify how I want to give feedback (currently using the Standard Rochio option).
true_user_story: The user shall optionally be prompted to provide a Feedback Method.  “Standard Rochio” is currently the only option.

user_story: As a user, I want to be able to cancel the project‑creation process at any time before the project is created, so that I can return to the previous state without committing any changes.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to cancel the process and return to the previous state at any time before the project is created.

user_story: As a project owner, I want to create a new project and specify its initial mode (Trace, Project Mode, or Assess) so that the project starts in the correct workflow context.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to create the project using the above input.  The project shall be placed in “Trace” mode.

user_story: As a user, I want to load an existing project so that I can continue working on it from the last saved state with all associated mode options.
true_user_story: Since the majority of projects are not completed in a single setting, then it is critical to provide the user the option to load an existing project to continue working on the project.  Load Project feature will provide the user the capacity to open an existing project in the last saved state with all the associated mode options. [1]

user_story: As a project manager, I want to create a new project by selecting a saved project from a list, entering a new project name, and specifying the low‑level document path, so that I can load the RTM and manage the project efficiently.
true_user_story: The user shall be prompted to select a project name from a list of saved projects.  Project names are obtained by scanning the saved projects directory.

user_story: As a user, I want to be optionally prompted to provide a feedback method, so that I can select the available option, "Standard Rochio", if I choose to give feedback.
true_user_story: The user shall be prompted to select a feedback method.  The only option is “Standard Rochio”.

user_story: As a user, I want to be able to cancel the load‑project action and return to the previous activity, and to load a project only when I have entered a project name and selected a feedback method, so that I can control the project creation process and avoid accidental or incomplete project creation.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to cancel the LOAD PROJECT action and return to the previous activity.

user_story: As a user, I want to be able to load an existing project or create a new one in Trace mode—providing the project name and feedback method—while also having the option to cancel the Load Project action and return to the previous activity, so that I can manage my projects efficiently and avoid accidental changes.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to load the project once the project name and feedback method is selected.

user_story: As a project manager, I want to load an existing RTM to initialize a new project, so that I can quickly set up the project with all requirements traceability data already in place and ensure accurate requirement tracking.
true_user_story: Similar in functionality to the START NEW PROJECT function, the LOAD RTM function collects project parameters from the user and initializes the project.  A project created via the LOAD RTM function may be in either “trace” or “assess” mode.  Assess mode is usually used when the user is provided a RTM and needs to evaluate its accuracy, completeness, and satisfaction level.

user_story: As a user, I want to be prompted to provide the new project name and the low‑level document path when I click the "Load RTM" button, so that I can create a new project with the correct information and start working on it immediately.
true_user_story: The user shall be prompted to provide the new project name.

user_story: As a project manager, I want the system to prompt me to provide the new project name, low‑level document path, high‑level document path, and RTM file path, so that I can configure the project setup accurately and efficiently.
true_user_story: The user shall be prompted to provide the low level document path.

user_story: As a project manager, I want the system to prompt me for the new project name, the high‑level document path, the low‑level document path, and the RTM file path, so that I can initialize a new project with all required documentation in place.
true_user_story: The user shall be prompted to provide the high level document path.

user_story: As a user, I want the system to prompt me to provide the high‑level requirements document path, the RTM file path, and optionally select an IR Method, so that I can correctly configure RETRO to process the requirements and traceability data.
true_user_story: The user shall be prompted to provide the RTM file path.

user_story: As a user, I want to be optionally prompted to select an IR method from a list of options (Vector‑Space Retrieval, Keyword Extraction, Keyword Extraction‑IDF), so that I can choose the method that best fits my information‑retrieval needs.
true_user_story: The user shall optionally be prompted to select an IR Method from the following list: Vector-Space Retrieval Keyword Extraction Keyword Extraction-IDF

user_story: As a user, I want to be prompted to select a Vocabulary Base of either “Both Documents” or “Low Level Document Only” for IDF keyword extraction, so that I can tailor the extraction to my needs with the default being “Both Documents”.
true_user_story: The user shall optionally be prompted to select a Vocabulary Base of either “Both Documents” or “Low Level Document Only”.  The default value is “Both Documents”.

user_story: As a user of RETRO, I want to be prompted to select a weight option (TF‑IDF, Okapi, or LTU) when I choose an IR method that requires weighting (Keyword Extraction or Keyword Extraction‑IDF), so that I can tailor the term‑weighting scheme to better suit my retrieval needs. The default weight should be TF‑IDF, but I should also have the option to choose Okapi or LTU if I prefer. For other IR methods, the weight selection should be optional and not mandatory.
true_user_story: The user shall optionally be prompted to provide “Weight Option” from the following list: TF-IDF (Default) Okapi LTU

user_story: As a user, I want the system to require me to set a weight threshold whenever I select the IR Method of Keyword Extraction or Keyword Extraction‑IDF, so that I can fine‑tune keyword relevance and ensure the search results are filtered appropriately.
true_user_story: If the user has selected an IR Method of “Keyword Extraction” or “Keyword Extraction-IDF”, the user must also select a “Weight Threshold”.

user_story: As a content creator, I want to be optionally prompted to provide a Thesaurus filename and a Stop Word filename, so that I can customize the thesaurus and stop‑word list used by the application to better suit my workflow.
true_user_story: The user shall optionally be prompted to provide a “Thesaurus” filename.

user_story: As a user, I want to be optionally prompted to provide a thesaurus filename and a stop‑word file, so that I can customize the text‑processing behavior to my specific needs.
true_user_story: The user shall optionally be prompted to provide the “Stop Word” filename.

user_story: As a user, I want to be optionally prompted to provide a Feedback Method, so that I can specify how I want to give feedback, currently using the Standard Rochio method.
true_user_story: The user shall optionally be prompted to provide a “Feedback Method”.  “Standard Rochio” is currently the only option.

user_story: As a user, I want to be prompted to select a project mode—either **Trace** (the default) or **Assess**—so that I can choose the appropriate workflow for evaluating a Requirements Traceability Matrix (RTM) and its accuracy, completeness, and satisfaction level.
true_user_story: The user shall optionally be prompted to provide the “Project Mode” of either “Trace” or “Assess”.  “Trace” is the default mode.

user_story: As a user, I want to be able to cancel the project creation process at any time before the project is created, so that I can return to the previous state without creating the project.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to cancel the process and return to the previous state at any time before the project is created.

user_story: As a user, I want to create a new project by entering the required fields and selecting a mode (Project, Assess, or Trace) so that the project is initialized in the correct workflow context and can be saved for future use.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to create the project using the above input.  The project shall be placed in specified “Project Mode”.

user_story: As a project manager, I want to activate the SAVE function to preserve the current data and status of my project, view a list of generated reports, and choose to save or modify the design of those reports, so that I can ensure all project information is backed up and reports are tailored to my needs.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to activate the SAVE function to save the current data and status of the project.

user_story: As a RETRO user, I want the application to automatically save all current data and project when I activate the save function, and to prompt me to save before closing the current project via the File menu, so that I never lose unsaved work and can exit the project safely. Additionally, I want to be able to view the project’s XML representation, so that I can inspect or export the underlying data structure.
true_user_story: When activated RETRO will save all current data and project.

user_story: As a RETRO user, I want to close the current project from the File menu without exiting the application, so that I can switch to another project or exit later without losing my work.
true_user_story: The CLOSE CURRENT PROJECT Function will allow the user to close a given project without exiting the RETRO application.

user_story: As a RETRO user, I want to close the current project, so that I can keep the application running and switch to another project or exit later without losing the current project’s state.
true_user_story: The user shall be able close the current project.

user_story: As a RETRO user, I want a clear mechanism to close the current project or exit the application, and to cancel project creation when prompted, so that I can finish my work safely and avoid unintended actions.
true_user_story: It is necessary to provide a mechanism to exit the RETRO application when the user is finished working with the application.

user_story: As a user, I want to be able to exit the RETRO application and cancel the current process to return to the previous state, so that I can stop work and revert changes before creating a project.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to exit the application.

user_story: As a user, I want to view the current project's XML file format by selecting the Show XML option from the Action menu, so that I can see a clear, XML‑formatted list of all high and low connections on a dedicated screen.
true_user_story: The user may want to view the current project’s XML file format.  The SHOW XML function will provide the user a display of the current XML file.

user_story: As a project manager, I want to view the XML version of the RTM and generate RTM reports by providing the high‑level document path and RTM file path, and choose the RTM With Text option, so that I can analyze and share the RTM data in a new screen.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to view the XML version of the RTM.

user_story: As a user, I want to view a list of automatically generated reports and have the option to save or modify the design of each report, so that I can manage and customize project outcomes after a trace or assess process is completed.
true_user_story: After a project has completed either a trace or an assess process, then the user will need the capability to review the final results of the process in an easy to read format other than the XML file format that is automatically generated throughout the process.  The GENERATE REPORT function will provide the user a list of reports that the user may view.  The user will have the option to save the generated report or modify the design of the report. [1]

user_story: As a user, I want to generate and save RTM reports—either with the RTM‑with‑Text option or the standard RTM option—and also generate and save a Parent‑with‑no‑children report, so that I can analyze traceability relationships and identify orphaned parent records in the system.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to generate and save the “RTM report”

user_story: As a data analyst, I want to generate and save the RTM report, Parent with no children report, Parents with no children with text report, and Children with no parents report from the Action menu, so that I can efficiently identify and document missing family relationships.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to generate and save the “Parent with no children report”

user_story: As a data analyst, I want to generate and save the Parents with no children with text report, the Children with no parents report, the Children with no parents with text report, and the Not satisfied requirements report from the Action menu (each report opens in a new screen), so that I can quickly identify missing relationships and unmet requirements and take corrective action.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to generate and save the “Parents with no children with text report”

user_story: As a project manager, I want to generate and save the RTM report, the parent‑with‑no‑children report, the parents‑with‑no‑children‑with‑text report, the children‑with‑no‑parents report, the children‑with‑no‑parents‑with‑text report, and the not‑satisfied‑requirements report, so that I can monitor traceability, identify missing parent‑child relationships, and ensure all requirements are satisfied.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to generate and save the “Children with no parents report”

user_story: As a system user, I want to generate and save the Parents with no children with text report, the Children with no parents report, the Children with no parents with text report, and the Not satisfied requirements report, so that I can identify data gaps, ensure compliance, and maintain data quality. The Parents report should open in a new screen, and I should be able to generate the Children With No Parents With Text report via the Action menu.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to generate and save the “Children with no parents with text report”

user_story: As a system analyst, I want to be able to generate and save multiple compliance reports—specifically a "Not satisfied requirements with text report", a "Children with no parents with text report", and a "Not satisfied requirements report"—and view them on a dedicated screen that includes a checkbox to filter for unsatisfied requirements, so that I can quickly identify gaps in requirements compliance and take corrective action.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to generate and save the “Not satisfied requirements report”

user_story: As a system analyst, I want to generate and save the "Not satisfied requirements with text report", the "Children with no parents with text report", and the "Not satisfied requirements report" from the Action menu, so that I can review unsatisfied requirements, identify orphaned child records, and assess overall requirement compliance in a single, auditable text file.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to generate and save the “Not satisfied requirements with text report”

user_story: As a requirements engineer, I want to limit the display of low‑level requirements or candidate requirement links, so that I can focus on the most relevant items while ensuring that no low‑level requirement is omitted from the candidate list.
true_user_story: “The user shall be able to limit the display of low-level requirements or candidate requirement links.” [1]

user_story: As a trace/assess user, I want to be able to select whether the filter process applies to the currently selected high‑level document element (LOCAL) or to all high‑level document elements (GLOBAL) by clicking the corresponding option on the Trace or Assess tab, so that I can control the scope of filtering for my analysis.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to designate whether the filter process applies to the selected high level document element (LOCAL) or to all high level document elements (GLOBAL).

user_story: As a requirements analyst, I want to filter the list of low‑level requirements by setting a weight cutoff (between 0 and 1.0), limit the display of low‑level requirements or candidate requirement links, and sort the remaining items using three different sort options, so that I can focus on the most relevant requirements and review them efficiently.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to filter the list of low-level requirements by setting a cutoff point for the weight between 0 and 1.0.

user_story: As a user, I want to designate the top N elements to display by selecting the “Show Top” radio option and entering a positive number, and I want the slider to be properly formatted and positioned to reflect a weight between 0 and 1.0, so that I can focus on the most relevant items and adjust the display intensity accurately.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to designate that the top N elements to be displayed.

user_story: As a user, I want to activate the **Kill Hidden Links** function (by clicking the “Kill Hidden Links” button) and the **Remove All No Links** action (via the Action menu), so that all elements that are not currently displayed or have no links are removed from the interface, keeping my workspace clean and focused on visible content.
true_user_story: The user shall be able activate a KILL HIDDEN Links function to remove all elements not currently displayed.

user_story: As a requirements analyst, I want to be able to sort low‑level requirement documents by **Requirement ID**, **Weights**, and a third attribute (e.g., **Priority**), so that I can quickly locate, compare, and review specific requirements in the DISPLAY function.
true_user_story: The user will need the ability to view the text of low-level requirements.  The DISPLAY function will provide three different sort options to view the text of low-level requirements.  The user can sort the low-level requirements based upon the weight of linking relationships, original document order, or by requirement id. [1]

user_story: As a user, I want to be able to sort low‑level documents by Requirement ID, Weights, and Document Order using the available radio options (including a dedicated ‘By Requirement’ option), so that I can view the documents in the order that best supports my analysis and prioritisation.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to sort the low level documents by “Requirement ID”

user_story: As a user, I want to sort low‑level documents by Requirement ID, Weights, and Document Order using the provided radio options, so that I can efficiently locate and manage documents based on my preferred criteria.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to sort the low level documents by “Weights”

user_story: As a user, I want to be able to sort low‑level documents by **Document Order**, **Requirement ID**, **Weights**, and **Requirement** (via the “By Requirement” radio option), so that I can quickly organize and locate documents according to the criteria that best suits my workflow.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to sort the low level documents by “Document Order”

user_story: As a user, I want to be able to run RETRO in **Assess** mode so that I can evaluate the accuracy and completeness of the tracing process and determine whether the tracing was successful, enabling me to provide feedback that improves the system.
true_user_story: Since RETRO’s tracing and assessing modes are based upon algorithms that will allow processes to be improved as the system receives FEEDBACK from the user.

user_story: As a requirement analyst, I want to change the link status of low‑level requirements (Link, No Link, Default) through a right‑click context menu and quickly delete any requirement that has a status of "No Link", so that I can keep the requirement hierarchy clean and accurately reflect current project dependencies.
true_user_story: The user shall select a low level requirement and identify the link status from the following list: Link No Link Default

user_story: As a developer, I want to load and keep multiple projects open at the same time, so that I can switch between and continue working on different projects without losing progress or context.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to load multiple projects at the same time.

user_story: As a RETRO application user, I want to open multiple projects simultaneously, resume an existing project in its last‑saved state with all mode options, and close any individual project without exiting the application, so that I can manage several projects concurrently while preserving my workflow and context.
true_user_story: The RETRO System shall allow multiple projects to be open at the same time

user_story: As a user, I want to import and load projects—where imported projects are automatically created in Trace mode—so that I can resume work immediately after a trace or assess process has completed.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to import projects.

user_story: As a RETRO system user, I want the system to prompt me for the file paths of high‑level and low‑level requirements and automatically convert existing input text files into the RETRO file format, so that I can quickly import my requirements into the system without manual re‑formatting.
true_user_story: The RETRO System shall provide a mechanism to convert existing input text files into RETRO file format.  (Not implemented.)

user_story: As a user, I want a stable, well‑spelled, intuitive GUI interface that lets me view each requirement individually, so that I can interact with RETRO efficiently without errors or crashes.
true_user_story: The user shall use a GUI INTERFACE to work with RETRO.

user_story: As a RETRO end‑user, I want a stable, correctly spelled GUI interface that lets me interact with the system without crashes, so that I can perform my tasks efficiently and confidently.
true_user_story: The RETRO System shall provide a GUI mechanism for the user to interact with the RETRO System.

user_story: As a requirements analyst, I want to use a keyword search function in browse mode to find low‑level requirements, so that I can quickly locate and link them to the appropriate high‑level requirements.
true_user_story: The user is provided the capability via BROWSE mode to manually link low-level requirements to high-level requirement. In order to help the user to quickly locate the desired low-level requirement or high-level requirement, a KEYWORD SEARCH function is offered to the user. [1]

user_story: As a requirements engineer, I want to enter a keyword into a search box to search both low‑level and high‑level requirements, so that I can quickly locate and trace the desired requirement.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to enter a keyword into a search text box to search within low-level requirements and the system shall return matches.

user_story: As a requirement analyst, I want to search high‑level and low‑level requirements by entering a keyword into a search text box, so that I can quickly locate the desired requirements.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to enter a keyword into a search text box to search within high-level requirements and the system shall return matches.

user_story: As a user, I want to manually link or trace items in BROWSE mode and delete any recommended links that I disagree with, so that I can correct RETRO’s candidate links and maintain accurate relationships in the system.
true_user_story: There will be times when the user will not agree with RETRO’s list of candidate links.  Therefore, it is important to provide a mechanism for the user to manually change, create, or delete links between low and high level requirements.  BROWSE mode is the mechanism within RETRO that enables the user to perform MANUAL LINKING/TRACING. [1]

user_story: As a requirements analyst, I want to limit the display of low‑level requirement links or candidate requirement links, so that I can focus on the most relevant high‑level requirement relationships and reduce visual clutter.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to create and remove low-level requirements with high-level requirements.

user_story: As a requirements engineer, I want to generate a trace for all or selected requirements, so that I can automatically see how high‑level requirements map to low‑level ones and verify the completeness and consistency of the requirement set.
true_user_story: One of the purposes of RETRO is to aid the user in linking low-level requirements with high-level requirements.  TRACING functionality is the component of RETRO that automatically generates a list of candidate links of high to low-level requirements.

user_story: As a requirement analyst, I want to generate a trace for all requirements or for a selected set of requirements, so that I can automatically view candidate links between high‑level and low‑level requirements and evaluate them efficiently.
true_user_story: RETRO shall provide a mechanism for the user to generate a trace on all requirements.

user_story: As a requirements analyst, I want to generate a trace for all requirements or for a selected set of requirements, so that I can automatically produce a list of candidate links between high‑level and low‑level requirements and evaluate them efficiently.
true_user_story: RETRO shall provide a mechanism for the user to generate a trace on a given requirement(s).

user_story: As a project stakeholder, I want to review the tracing results, provide feedback, and complete the tracing process, so that the project is marked as completed, no further changes are allowed, and future tracing can be improved.
true_user_story: After the TRACING function is performed, then the results of the TRACING process will need to be made available to the user to view.  The TRACING RESULTS functionality will display a list of all candidate links of high to low-level requirements based on any given filtering criteria.  The user will be allowed to provide feedback on the TRACING RESULTS in order to improve future TRACING.

user_story: As a researcher, I want RETRO to display a list of candidate links generated from tracing, along with a clear satisfaction indicator for each link, and to allow me to provide feedback and apply filters to that list, so that I can quickly assess which links meet my needs and decide whether to accept them or manually adjust the linking.
true_user_story: RETRO shall display the list of candidate links based on the tracing and permit feedback, filtering, and display functionality.

user_story: As a trace engineer, I want to save the current status of the trace, so that I can preserve my work and resume it later without losing any data.
true_user_story: The SAVE TRACE function shall allow the user to save the trace result.

user_story: As a requirements engineer, I want to generate a trace for all requirements or a selected set, with the default mode set to Trace and an optional Assess mode, and be able to save the current status of the trace, so that I can maintain up‑to‑date traceability records and resume work without losing progress.
true_user_story: RETRO shall provide a mechanism to save the current status of trace.

user_story: As a trace analyst, I want to complete the tracing process and save the current status of the trace, so that the project is marked as completed and no further changes can be made, ensuring trace integrity and a reliable final snapshot.
true_user_story: The COMPLETING TRACE function shall allow user to finish the tracing process.

user_story: As a project team member, I want RETRO to automatically save all current data and the project when it is activated and to provide a simple way to close the current project, so that I can reliably link low‑level requirements to high‑level releases without risking data loss or having to manually manage project files.
true_user_story: RETRO shall mark the project completed and not permit further changes.

user_story: As a requirements analyst, I want a mechanism that runs accuracy and completeness tests on tracing results, displays candidate links sorted from high‑ to low‑level requirements, allows filtering by any given criteria, and lets me provide feedback on the results, so that I can confirm the tracing was successful and continuously improve future tracing efforts.
true_user_story: The user needs a mechanism that will help to determine whether the TRACING was successfully performed based on accuracy and completeness.  An accuracy test tells the user that the candidate links provided are correctly related to the high-level requirement.  Completeness test tells the user that all possible links that are listed in the candidate list are included and no low-level requirements are missing. [1]

user_story: As a requirements engineer, I want to run an Accuracy & Completeness check from the Assess tab by clicking the AccuracyCompleteness button, so that I can verify that the IR method results match the RTM and improve the accuracy and completeness of the Requirements Traceability Matrix, receiving recommended links for any gaps.
true_user_story: RETRO shall provide a mechanism for the user to be able to run an ACCURACY & COMPLETENESS check by performing a full trace on the requirements and comparing the IR method results to the given RTM. [1]

user_story: As a user, I want to see a list of recommendations generated by RETRO’s tracing process while in assess mode, and be able to give feedback on whether the results align with the RTM, so that I can ensure accurate requirement traceability and help the system improve its recommendation quality.
true_user_story: “The [user] will need to have a list of recommendations based on RETRO’s TRACING process.  SHOW RECOMMENDATION will display to the [user] a list of recommendation while in ASSESS mode.  The [user] will have the capability to provide FEEDBACK to the RETRO system on whether or not the [user] agrees with RETRO’s assessment of the linking between the low-level requirements to the high-level requirements.” [1]

user_story: As a user, I want to be able to display or hide the list of recommendations generated by RETRO’s TRACING process while in ASSESS mode, and to provide feedback on each recommendation, so that I can evaluate the suggestions and influence the system’s future recommendations.
true_user_story: RETRO shall display/hide recommendations at the user’s request.

user_story: As a requirement analyst, I want a satisfaction analysis function that displays the percentage of satisfaction for each high‑level requirement against its linked low‑level requirements, and automatically updates the status to "Satisfied", "Partially Satisfied" or "Not Satisfied", so that I can quickly assess compliance, identify gaps and prioritize corrective actions.
true_user_story: Satisfaction function shows how satisfied the high-level requirement is with the given set of low-level requirements. [4] The Satisfaction Analysis function is checking the level of percentage for a satisfaction of the linked result with the high and low level input files. Once final output is agreed, the list of high level requirement is updated with options like “Satisfied”, “Not Satisfied” and “Partially Satisfied”. The status of high level requirement is color coded. Unsatisfied result can be repeated until all the high levels has been satisfied stage.

user_story: As a user, I want RETRO to display the percentage level of satisfaction for each low‑level candidate link when I run the Satisfaction button, so that I can quickly identify the level of satisfaction of each link.
true_user_story: RETRO shall display to the user the percentage level of link. [1]

user_story: Remove all low‑level requirements that have no link to a high‑level requirement while in ASSESS mode.
true_user_story: While in ASSESS mode, the user will need a mechanism to allow the removal of all low-level requirements that are not linked to a high-level requirement.  REMOVE ALL NO LINKS function will provide an easy way for the user to quickly remove low-level requirements with status of “No Link”.

user_story: As a user, I want to be able to remove all links marked as "No Link" and delete recommended links, so that my link list remains relevant and clutter‑free.
true_user_story: RETRO shall remove all links marked as “No Link”

user_story: As a user in ASSESS mode, I want to delete recommended links that are not correctly linked and remove all low‑level requirements that are not connected to a high‑level requirement, so that the requirement hierarchy remains accurate, clean, and reflects only valid relationships.
true_user_story: While in ASSESS mode, the user will need a mechanism that will allow the deletion of recommended links that the user feels are not correctly linked.

user_story: As a user of RETRO, I want the system to automatically remove all links marked as “No Link” and allow me to select and delete recommended links, so that my link list remains clean, relevant, and easy to manage.
true_user_story: RETRO shall permit the user to select a recommended link and delete it.

user_story: As a user, I want RETRO to color‑code the satisfaction status of each candidate link, so that I can quickly identify the satisfaction level of each link and make decisions more efficiently.
true_user_story: “In order to help the user to quickly identify the level of satisfaction of each candidate link, RETRO will need to provide a visual aid.  COLOR-CODED SATISFACTION STATUS function will color code each candidate link based on its satisfaction level.   The various colors will be a visual aid to the user to help quickly identify each links satisfaction level.” [1]

user_story: As a RETRO user, I want each candidate link to be color‑coded according to its satisfaction status, so that I can quickly identify and prioritize links visually without having to read detailed text.
true_user_story: RETRO shall COLOR-CODE THE SATISFACTION STATUS of each given candidate link.
